input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to `.runsettings` after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me first look at their existing files to understand the current configuration, and also invoke the relevant skill for MSTest migration guidance.

data.content:
The `.testsettings` format is **deprecated** — modern MSTest (v2+) ignores it. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's your configuration mapped over:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property | `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| `AssemblyResolution` | `<DeploymentEnabled>` (deployment is off by default now) |
| Code Coverage DataCollector | `<DataCollectors>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout in milliseconds -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces the old AssemblyResolution / DeploymentEnabled behavior.
         Set to true only if your tests rely on deployed files. -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <!-- Add include/exclude module filters here if needed -->
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Test deployment/assembly resolution settings
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file since `.testsettings` is deprecated/ignored in modern MSTest.

Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file for them.

arguments.file_text:

<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout in milliseconds -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces the old AssemblyResolution / DeploymentEnabled behavior.
         Set to true only if your tests rely on deployed files. -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <!-- Add include/exclude module filters here if needed -->
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
I've created **`local.runsettings`** with the equivalent configuration. To use it:

**CLI:**
```bash
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```

**Or wire it into the `.csproj` so it's always used:**
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**Key differences from `.testsettings`:**

1. **Deployment** is off by default in modern MSTest — only enable it if tests depend on copied files.
2. **Timeout** moves from `<Properties>` into `<RunConfiguration><TestTimeout>`.
3. **Code Coverage** collector URI changed to `2.0`; the old `1.0` URI is no longer recognized.

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` once you've confirmed the new settings work.
